chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الدُّعَاءِ لِلْكَرْبِ وَ الْهَمِّ وَ الْحُزْنِ وَ الْخَوْفِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 55 – The supplication for the distress, and the worries, and the grief, and the fear

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/426)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/426)
text: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ
يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّومُ يَا لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ بِرَحْمَتِكَ أَسْتَغِيثُ فَاكْفِنِي مَا أَهَمَّنِي وَ لَا تَكِلْنِي إِلَى نَفْسِي تَقُولُهُ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ وَ أَنْتَ سَاجِدٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Umar Bin Yazeed,

‘O the Living! O the Eternal! There is no god except Youazwj. By Yourazwj Mercy I seek Help, therefore Suffice me for what worries me, and not Leave me to myself’ – you should say it one hundred times while you are in Sajdah’.
en.sarwar: 20. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from ‘Umar ibn Yazid who has said the following: “O the Living, O the Guardian, O the One besides Whom no one deserves to be worshipped, I cry to You for help through Your mercy, suffice me whatever concerns me and do not leave me to myself.” One should say it one hundred times in prostrating position.
